bedroom_observations: The curtain is thin enough for the morning to arrive before anyone calls your name.
A bus exhales somewhere downhill. A dog answers from behind a metal gate.
Your notebook waits on the chair with yesterday's corner still bent.
threshold_observations: The sidewalk outside is already wet in patches, smelling faintly of soap and dust.
Wires cross the pale sky in patient lines. A neighbor's radio leaks through a half-open window.
The gate clicks shut behind you, small and final.

breakfast_choices.label: Pao com manteiga
breakfast_choices.kitchen_observation: The butter melts unevenly, leaving one corner almost dry and another shining.
breakfast_choices.threshold_consequence: Warm bread stays with you at the gate. The street feels a little easier to enter.

breakfast_choices.label: Banana from the fruit bowl
breakfast_choices.kitchen_observation: The banana has one soft bruise that looks worse than it tastes.
breakfast_choices.threshold_consequence: Your fingertips still carry the peel's green smell when you touch the gate latch.

breakfast_choices.label: Leave the plate alone
breakfast_choices.kitchen_observation: The empty plate keeps its place on the table, catching the window light.
breakfast_choices.threshold_consequence: At the gate, your stomach makes a private argument with the morning traffic.
